name: sprayingtoolkit
title: sprayingtoolkit

commands.name: atomizer

arguments.description: csv file containing usernames and passwords
arguments.raw: -c, --csvfile CSVFILE    csv file containing usernames and passwords
-i, --interval TIME      spray at the specified interval [format: "H:M:S"]

arguments.description: number of concurrent threads to use [default: 3]
arguments.raw: -t, --threads THREADS    number of concurrent threads to use [default: 3]

arguments.description: target port of the IMAP server (IMAP only) [default: 993]
arguments.raw: -p, --targetPort PORT    target port of the IMAP server (IMAP only) [default: 993]

arguments.description: only collect info, don't password spray
arguments.raw: --recon                  only collect info, don't password spray
--gchat URL              gchat webhook url for notification
--slack URL              slack webhook url for notification

arguments.description: username row title in CSV file [default: Email Address]
arguments.raw: --user-row-name NAME     username row title in CSV file [default: Email Address]

arguments.description: password row title in CSV file [default: Password]
arguments.raw: --pass-row-name NAME     password row title in CSV file [default: Password]

arguments.description: use the usernames in the specified file as the password (one per line)
arguments.raw: --user-as-pass USERFILE  use the usernames in the specified file as the password (one per line)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# atomizer -h
 Usage:
   atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> <password> <userfile> [--targetPort PORT] [--threads THREADS] [--debug]
   atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> <passwordfile> <userfile> --interval <TIME> [--gchat <URL>] [--slack <URL>] [--targetPort PORT][--threads THREADS] [--debug]
   atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> --csvfile CSVFILE [--user-row-name NAME] [--pass-row-name NAME] [--targetPort PORT] [--threads THREADS] [--debug]
   atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> --user-as-pass USERFILE [--targetPort PORT] [--threads THREADS] [--debug]
   atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> --recon [--debug]
   atomizer -h | --help
   atomizer -v | --version
 
 Arguments:
   target         target domain or url
   password       password to spray
   userfile       file containing usernames (one per line)
   passwordfile   file containing passwords (one per line)
 
 Options:
   -h, --help               show this screen
   -v, --version            show version
   -c, --csvfile CSVFILE    csv file containing usernames and passwords
   -i, --interval TIME      spray at the specified interval [format: "H:M:S"]
   -t, --threads THREADS    number of concurrent threads to use [default: 3]
   -d, --debug              enable debug output
   -p, --targetPort PORT    target port of the IMAP server (IMAP only) [default: 993]
   --recon                  only collect info, don't password spray
   --gchat URL              gchat webhook url for notification
   --slack URL              slack webhook url for notification
   --user-row-name NAME     username row title in CSV file [default: Email Address]
   --pass-row-name NAME     password row title in CSV file [default: Password]
   --user-as-pass USERFILE  use the usernames in the specified file as the password (one per line)

examples.command: atomizer (lync|owa|imap) <target> --csvfile CSVFILE [--user-row-name NAME] [--pass-row-name NAME] [--targetPort PORT] [--threads THREADS] [--debug]

commands.name: spindrift

arguments.description: optional domain or url to retrieve the internal domain name from OWA
arguments.raw: --target TARGET   optional domain or url to retrieve the internal domain name from OWA

arguments.description: manually specify the domain to append to each username
arguments.raw: --domain DOMAIN   manually specify the domain to append to each username

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# spindrift -h
 Usage:
   spindrift [<file>] [--target TARGET | --domain DOMAIN] [--format FORMAT]
 
 Arguments:
   file    file containing names, can also read from stdin
 
 Options:
   --target TARGET   optional domain or url to retrieve the internal domain name from OWA
   --domain DOMAIN   manually specify the domain to append to each username
   --format FORMAT   username format [default: {f}{last}]

local_help.--help:
Usage:
    spindrift [<file>] [--target TARGET | --domain DOMAIN] [--format FORMAT]

Arguments:
    file    file containing names, can also read from stdin

Options:
    --target TARGET   optional domain or url to retrieve the internal domain name from OWA
    --domain DOMAIN   manually specify the domain to append to each username
    --format FORMAT   username format [default: {f}{last}]
local_help.-h:
Usage:
    spindrift [<file>] [--target TARGET | --domain DOMAIN] [--format FORMAT]

Arguments:
    file    file containing names, can also read from stdin

Options:
    --target TARGET   optional domain or url to retrieve the internal domain name from OWA
    --domain DOMAIN   manually specify the domain to append to each username
    --format FORMAT   username format [default: {f}{last}]
local_help.help:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/spindrift", line 42, in <module>
    convert_to_ad_username(line, args['--format'], domain)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/bin/spindrift", line 22, in convert_to_ad_username
    first, last = name.strip().split()
    ^^^^^^^^^^^
ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 2)

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: kali-defaults, mitmproxy, python3-boto3, python3-docopt, python3-imapclient, python3-lxml, python3-requests, python3-requests-ntlm, python3-termcolor, python3-urllib3, python3:any
apt_metadata.description:
Scripts to make password spraying attacks against Lync/S4B, OWA & O365
A set of Python scripts/utilities that tries to make password spraying attacks
against Lync/S4B & OWA a lot quicker, less painful and more efficient.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use atomizer because ### sprayingtoolkit.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair atomizer with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `atomizer --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### sprayingtoolkit.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sprayingtoolkit
